$comment: THE CONTRACT between demo.html and the demo-server seeding task. Every Honua endpoint, service id, layer id, and OGC collection id the demo page touches is declared here and ONLY here. Seeded 2026-06-12 against the live demo server; this file reflects as-built reality. Deltas from the original contract, all forced by Honua server behavior: (1) FeatureServer layer indexes are the server-assigned publication ids (1..6), not 0 — Honua's layer-publish API numbers layers globally; (2) the MVT source-layer inside every tile is the constant 'layer' (PostGIS ST_AsMVT), while the OGC Tiles collection id in the URL still equals service.serviceId (the server resolves collections by resource name); (3) PostGIS-raster tiles are served by the ImageServer tile route, not MapServer; (4) elevation is served by Honua's /terrain/{dataset}/{z}/{x}/{y}.png route with Mapbox Terrain-RGB encoding (not terrarium, note x before y); UPDATE 2026-06-11: (3) and (4) are now documented FALLBACKS — the raster bases and the terrain layer each carry a 'pmtiles' block pointing at pre-baked static PMTiles archives served by the /api/v1/tiles/pmtiles/{id} range proxy (terrain re-encoded as terrarium), which demo.js prefers; the dynamic ImageServer//terrain routes remain seeded to demonstrate live rendering; UPDATE 2026-06-12: the MVT vector layers (parcels, zoning, roads — and maui-buildings on /demo-maui-3d.html) carry the same 'pmtiles' block pattern pointing at pre-baked tippecanoe archives (maui-*-static), so default map browsing never touches PostGIS — the dynamic /ogc/tiles routes stay seeded as the documented live-rendering lane (remove a pmtiles block to demonstrate it); (5) the glyph fontstack is the space-free 'NotoSansRegular' (API Gateway HTTP proxy to S3 rejects %20 path segments). CORS for https://honua.io is live on /rest/*, /ogc/*, /api/v1/tiles/* (app-level allowlist) and /fonts/* (bucket CORS, wildcard). UPDATE (basemap switcher): hillshade and imagery are no longer toggleable overlay entries in 'layers' — they belong to the EXCLUSIVE base definitions in 'bases' below, driven by the SDK's <honua-basemap-switcher> control, so hillshade and imagery can never stack. Data overlays (parcels/zoning/flood/SLR/places/roads) remain independent toggles above whichever base is active.
$basemapSeedingContract: SEEDED 2026-06-12 (Maui Nui): maui-basemap.pmtiles extracted from https://build.protomaps.com/20260610.pmtiles with --bbox=-157.40,20.45,-155.95,21.25 (all of Maui County: Maui, Molokaʻi, Lānaʻi, Kahoʻolawe) and uploaded to s3://honua-demo-data-585192672263/maui-basemap (ContentType application/vnd.pmtiles, metadata operation=publish — both required by the PMTiles range proxy). Served live at https://demo.honua.io/api/v1/tiles/pmtiles/maui-basemap.

server.$glyphsComment: Contract: the server serves SDF glyph PBFs for the font stack 'NotoSansRegular' at this route (API Gateway proxies /fonts/* to the demo data bucket; the space-free stack name is deliberate — API Gateway HTTP proxy integrations reject %20 path segments with a 505 from S3).

map.$comment: Framed for Maui Nui open data — the full Maui County extent (-157.40,20.45,-155.95,21.25): Maui, Molokaʻi, Lānaʻi, Kahoʻolawe.

basemap.$comment: Protomaps OSM basemap via Honua PMTiles proxy. Loaded beneath all data layers. Gracefully absent until the archive is seeded (demo.js catches 404 and stays on background color). Attribution appended to MapLibre attribution control when the basemap loads.

style.$comment: Minimal dark style targeting Protomaps v4 tile schema. Palette matches --bed- CSS variables: bg #08131a, surface #0e1e27, teal #3aa088, text-dim #8ea7a6. Layers ordered: earth → water → landuse → roads (3 classes) → boundaries → places.

bases.label: Map
bases.$comment: The dark Protomaps vector base. Sources/layers come from the 'basemap' block above; demo.js assembles the HonuaBasemapDefinition and hands it to <honua-basemap-switcher>.

bases.label: Imagery

pmtiles.$comment: PRIMARY SOURCE (seeded 2026-06-11): pre-baked WebP (RGBA, q75) tile pyramid, zooms 7-13 (native 20 m NAIP ≈ z13), built from the seeded NAIP RGB COG and uploaded to s3://honua-demo-data-585192672263/maui-imagery-static (ContentType application/vnd.pmtiles, metadata operation=publish). The downsampled overviews also fix the county-wide z≤8 view that the dynamic route could not render inside its 60 s budget.

service.$comment: FALLBACK / live-rendering alternative, kept seeded and documented on purpose: ImageServer renders tiles dynamically from PostGIS raster (usable z9+; the z≤8 county-wide mosaic exceeds the 60 s request budget). demo.js prefers the pmtiles block above.

bases.attribution: USDA NAIP · Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS Program

bases.label: Terrain
bases.$comment: Composite base: the dark vector base PLUS the hillshade raster, shown together as ONE exclusive option. The 3D DEM source for tilt is separate (the 'terrain' entry in 'layers' below) — it is elevation, not a display base, so it stays an independent toggle.

pmtiles.$comment: PRIMARY SOURCE (seeded 2026-06-11): pre-baked PNG (gray+alpha) tile pyramid, zooms 7-13 (native 20 m ≈ z13; MapLibre overzooms past that), built from the seeded USGS 3DEP hillshade COG and uploaded to s3://honua-demo-data-585192672263/maui-hillshade-static (ContentType application/vnd.pmtiles, metadata operation=publish — both required by the PMTiles range proxy). Static range reads: no dynamic rendering, no database.

service.$comment: FALLBACK / live-rendering alternative, kept seeded and documented on purpose: ImageServer renders these tiles dynamically from PostGIS raster (ST_Clip per request). demo.js prefers the pmtiles block above; remove it to demonstrate live rendering. ImageServer (not MapServer): Honua's MapServer tile route renders vector features.

pmtiles.$comment: PRIMARY SOURCE (seeded 2026-06-11): pre-baked terrarium-encoded RGB PNG tiles, zooms 7-12, generated per-zoom from the 20 m USGS 3DEP DEM (never averaged in RGB space — that corrupts encoded elevations) and uploaded to s3://honua-demo-data-585192672263/maui-terrain-static (ContentType application/vnd.pmtiles, metadata operation=publish). Decode check vs the source DEM: worst |error| 0.002 m. Encoding here is terrarium ((R*256+G+B/256)-32768); the dynamic fallback below stays mapbox.

service.$comment: FALLBACK / live-rendering alternative, kept seeded and documented on purpose: Honua's dedicated terrain route (/terrain/{dataset}/{z}/{x}/{y}.png — note x/y order) encodes Mapbox Terrain-RGB per request from PostGIS raster. demo.js prefers the pmtiles block above (note the different encodings). service.path stays on MapServer for the availability probe when this fallback is active.

layers.name: Sea level rise (3.2 ft)
layers.$paintComment: Distinct turquoise so the SLR extent never reads as a FEMA flood class (the flood layer owns the blue family). demo.js reads fill-color from here for the legend row — one declaration, two consumers.

layers.name: Flood hazard zones
layers.$paintComment: Paint is built at runtime by demo.js from its FLOOD_CLASSES palette constant (a match expression on fld_zone: V/VE coastal high hazard, A/AE/AH/AO 1%-annual-chance, X minimal, D undetermined). The same constant feeds the <honua-legend> section — one constant, two consumers, no drift. Discovered distinct fld_zone values on the live layer: A, AE, AH, AO, D, V, VE, X.

layers.name: Zoning

pmtiles.$comment: PRIMARY SOURCE (seeded 2026-06-12): pre-baked MVT pyramid (tippecanoe -l layer, z7-13, attrs zone_code/zone_dist/cp_area, 320 tiles, 1.8 MB) built from the same staged Maui Nui FlatGeobuf that seeded the live layer (3,274 districts) and uploaded to s3://honua-demo-data-585192672263/maui-zoning-static (ContentType application/vnd.pmtiles, metadata operation=publish — both required by the PMTiles range proxy). sourceLayer is the tippecanoe-assigned internal layer name, kept as 'layer' to match the server's ST_AsMVT convention so paints/legend are identical on both lanes. The dynamic OGC Tiles route below stays seeded as the documented live-rendering fallback; demo.js prefers this block.

tiles.$maxzoomComment: Source maxzoom: MapLibre overzooms beyond this instead of requesting deeper tiles — a z14 scene then needs ~2 z13 tiles, not ~18 z14 tiles, which the db.t4g.micro demo database cannot generate cold in parallel.
tiles.$sourceLayerComment: Honua's PostGIS MVT generator names the embedded vector-tile layer 'layer' (ST_AsMVT constant) regardless of collection id.

layers.$paintComment: Paint is built at runtime by demo.js from its ZONING_FAMILIES palette constant: a match expression on zone_code (the stable county district code — zone_dist spelling varies, e.g. 'M-1'/'M1') mapping each code to its district-family color. The same constant feeds the <honua-legend> explicit section — one constant, two consumers, no drift. Codes discovered via returnDistinctValues on the live layer.
layers.attribution: County of Maui · Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS Program

layers.name: Parcels (TMK)

pmtiles.$comment: PRIMARY SOURCE (seeded 2026-06-12): pre-baked MVT pyramid (tippecanoe -l layer, z10-13, --drop-densest-as-needed --detect-shared-borders, attrs tmk_txt/zone/gisacres/taxacres/qpub_link, 334 tiles, 9.0 MB) built from the same staged FlatGeobuf that seeded the live layer (51,245 parcels) and uploaded to s3://honua-demo-data-585192672263/maui-parcels-static (ContentType application/vnd.pmtiles, metadata operation=publish). This is THE structural fix for the cache-cold MVT bursts: a county pan now reads S3 byte ranges instead of fanning out ST_AsMVT queries against db.t4g.micro. Dynamic OGC Tiles route below = documented live-rendering fallback.

layers.$paintComment: Parcels render as thin lot lines (not fills) so the zoning families stay legible underneath; width/opacity scale with zoom because TMK parcels are extremely dense at z12.
layers.attribution: Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS Program · County of Maui

pmtiles.$comment: PRIMARY SOURCE (seeded 2026-06-12): pre-baked MVT pyramid (tippecanoe -l layer, z7-13, attrs fullname/streetname/streettype, 263 tiles, 1.1 MB) from the staged FlatGeobuf that seeded the live layer (7,071 centerlines; roadclass is NULL across the whole source dataset so tippecanoe correctly emits no such attribute — the demo styles roads with constants and popups query the FeatureServer, so nothing reads it). Uploaded to s3://honua-demo-data-585192672263/maui-roads-static (ContentType application/vnd.pmtiles, metadata operation=publish). Dynamic OGC Tiles route below = documented live-rendering fallback.

layers.name: Place names
layers.attribution: USGS GNIS · Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS Program
